title: Austria Faces World Cup Realigned by Baumgartner Injury
original_text:
The Austrian national football team’s World Cup preparations have been
fundamentally altered following confirmation that attacking midfielder Christoph
Baumgartner faces a significantly longer recovery period than initially
projected. The 26-year-old RB Leipzig
[https://roundtable.io/sports/soccer/bundesliga/rb-leipzig] star was forced to
withdraw from the squad after sustaining an injury during a warm-up session in a
match against Tunisia. Subsequent medical assessments and a surgical procedure
have revealed the full extent of the damage, reshaping Austria's tactical
outlook just days before their tournament opener.

According to a report from Krone [https://www.krone.at/4170388], the injury is
more severe than first anticipated, with the Austrian outlet stating that
Baumgartner could be sidelined for up to five months. This extended recovery
timeline rules him out of the entire World Cup tournament and threatens his
availability for the start of the 2026/27 campaign with RB Leipzig
[https://roundtable.io/sports/soccer/bundesliga/rb-leipzig]. The specific
diagnosis involves a proximal rectus femoris tendon injury in the hip.

The medical procedure was performed in Turku, Finland, by specialist Lasse
Lempainen. Following the surgery, Lempainen shared an update via Instagram
[https://www.instagram.com/p/DZU9VPsNrom/?utm_source=ig_embed], posting a
photograph alongside the player. In the post, the specialist noted that missing
a World Cup due to an injury is difficult for any player and extended wishes for
a speedy recovery and a strong return to the field. Lempainen expressed
gratitude toward Baumgartner, RB Leipzig
[https://roundtable.io/sports/soccer/bundesliga/rb-leipzig], and the Austrian
Football Association (ÖFB) for their trust and cooperation, adding that while
getting back on the field is a milestone, returning to best form is the real
goal.

In the wake of the medical update, Austria team boss Ralf Rangnick has opted for
a patient approach regarding his squad composition. According to Krone Sport
[https://www.krone.at/4164797], Rangnick made the decision not to immediately
name a replacement for the injured offensive all-rounder. Under FIFA
regulations, member associations are permitted to nominate a replacement player
in the event of an injury up to 24 hours before their opening match. Austria is
scheduled to begin its World Cup campaign in Santa Clara against Jordan on June
16 local time. Rangnick intends to utilize the remaining time to assess the
squad for any additional needs or minor injuries that may arise during their
preparation camp in California.

The absence leaves a notable void in the Austrian midfield, a sentiment echoed
within the squad. According to OneFootball [https://www.krone.at/4164797],
teammate Konrad Laimer stated that the injury hurts the team a lot and noted
that Baumgartner will be missed throughout the World Cup. Laimer acknowledged
that the midfielder cannot be replaced on a one-to-one basis, meaning the squad
must attempt to absorb the absence collectively.

To fill the structural void in the offensive center, Austria retains several
options within the current roster. According to Krone Sport
[https://www.krone.at/4164797], Marcel Sabitzer, Sasa Kalajdzic, and youngsters
Carney Chukwuemeka and Paul Wanner are among the candidates who could assume
Baumgartner's responsibilities on the pitch. Despite being unable to play,
Baumgartner intends to travel to the United States to support his teammates from
the sidelines, acting as a presence for the squad throughout the tournament. The
stage only gets bigger from here.
_note: 全文走 summary 块A(LLM三判后只增不改);processed=成熟仓库累积,raw=本次收集过程。
